mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and multiple other tyrosine kinases, blocking proliferative signaling in AML cells
Inhibits RAF kinases and multiple receptor tyrosine kinases including FLT3, VEGFR, and PDGFR
Inhibits FLT3 and other receptor tyrosine kinases involved in proliferation and survival
Inhibits BCR-ABL, Src family kinases, KIT and other kinases, disrupting oncogenic signaling
Selectively inhibits BCR-ABL and can inhibit some FLT3 and KIT mutants
Potently inhibits BCR-ABL (including T315I), FLT3-ITD and other AML-relevant kinases
Covalently inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase, with additional off-target effects on myeloid signaling pathways
Selectively inhibits anti-apoptotic protein BCL-2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, reducing JAK/STAT pathway signaling
Allosterically inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and cell growth
Prodrug of sirolimus; inhibits mTORC1 signaling to block cell growth and proliferation
Inhibits mitochondrial complex I, activates AMPK, and induces metabolic stress in leukemic cells
Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, in the presence of copper, affects proteasome and NF-κB signaling leading to apoptosis
additional_notes: Tested in combination with fixed low micromolar copper (e.g., CuCl₂ or CuSO₄ at ~0.5–1 µM)

mechanism: Lysosomotropic agent that impairs autophagosome-lysosome fusion, inhibiting autophagy
additional_notes: Alternative: hydroxychloroquine with similar autophagy-inhibitory mechanism

mechanism: Primarily inhibits COX-2; also exerts COX-2–independent effects on PI3K/Akt and ER stress pathways
Inhibits histone deacetylases leading to altered gene expression, differentiation, and growth inhibition
Inhibits class I and II histone deacetylases, inducing apoptosis and differentiation in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA (suberoylanilide hydroxamic acid)

mechanism: Binds cereblon E3 ligase complex leading to degradation of IKZF1/3 and immunomodulatory/anti-angiogenic effects
additional_notes: Part of the thalidomide/lenalidomide IMiD class

category: Oral gold compound; thioredoxin reductase inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing ROS and triggering apoptosis in AML cells
Inhibits dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH), blocking de novo pyrimidine synthesis and cell proliferation
additional_notes: Active metabolite of leflunomide; leflunomide itself may require mid- to high-micromolar concentrations in vitro
